# 5 — ChatGPT Context

**Phase.** Fifth (in loop terms; cycles with stages 2 and 4).

**Produces.** Rendered Meta static ad images, generated by ChatGPT from structured image prompts produced by the Ideator (batch initial render) or the Critic (per-creative regen).

**Inputs.** One of two prompt formats:

- **Format A — Batch initial prompts** from `2-Ideation/SKILL.md`. ChatGPT receives ~25-30 of these in one session and renders the full creative concept set.
- **Format B — Single regeneration prompt** from `4-Critique/SKILL.md`. ChatGPT receives one of these per per-creative iteration; includes a `CONTEXT` block declaring v1 failures so v2 specifically addresses them.

Both formats are defined in `Services/Meta-Ads/Meta-Ads_Methodology.md` §7.

**Outputs.** Image (rendered) + a 1-2 line description + flags from the pre-render sanity check + AI-tell self-assessment. For batch sessions, output one short reply per concept rather than 25-30 long ones.

**Files in this folder.**

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `chatgpt-image-gen-context.md` | The doc loaded into the ChatGPT custom GPT or Project as system context. Teaches ChatGPT how to read both prompt formats, how to handle batch render sessions, what its role is, and what it does and does not do. |

**Why this folder exists.** ChatGPT runs the image-generation half of the loop. Without shared vocabulary, the loop breaks: Claude says "TOF cold, primary lever LF3, concept #14 of 30" and ChatGPT has no idea what those mean. This folder is the canonical reference that keeps both sides aligned.

**Why Claude reads this too.** Claude (as Ideator, Producer, or Critic) needs to understand what its downstream consumer (ChatGPT) is configured to do. If Claude knows ChatGPT will not invent text overlays, it produces prompts with explicit `TEXT OVERLAY: none` instructions. If Claude knows ChatGPT defaults to 4:5 when the field is missing, it can rely on that default or override it explicitly.

**Setup (one-time).** Load `chatgpt-image-gen-context.md` into:
- A ChatGPT custom GPT (paste into "Instructions"), or
- A ChatGPT Project (upload as Knowledge file), or
- Pasted at the top of a chat session before any image generation prompts

**Trigger phrases that route here.** "Image generation", "ChatGPT prompt", "render the image", "DALL-E reference", "batch render", "what should ChatGPT see."

**Known gaps.**

- Custom GPT vs. Project decision is open. Custom GPT is shareable; Project is per-user. Decide before client work scales.
- No tracked changelog for the ChatGPT context doc — when frameworks update in the methodology, both sides need to sync. Add a "last updated" footer or link to a changelog in `/CLEANUP.md`.
- Batch render sessions of 25-30 prompts may hit ChatGPT's session-length or rate limits depending on plan. Test before relying on a single-session batch for production runs.
